component.description: 4.5V to 72V, 2A, DC/DC Synchronous Step-Down Regulator with Internal Compensation and Programmable Frequency

absolute_maximum_ratings.raw_name: Human Body Model (HBM) ESD Stress Voltage
Charged Device Model (CDM) ESD Stress Voltage

pin_definitions.description: These pins are connected to the source of the integrated low-side FET and are used as the power ground. Connect these pins to VIN with input bypass capacitors.
These pins are the phase node of the regulator. They are connected to the source of the high-side FET and the drain of the low-side FET. Connect these pins to the inductor and the boot capacitor.

This pin is connected to the drain of the integrated high-side FET. This pin is also connected to the input of internal linear regulator that provides bias for the IC. Connect this pin to the input rail and to PGND with input bypass capacitors.
This pin is the bootstrap circuit supply pin. Connect this pin to SW with a capacitor to program the soft-start time. Tie this pin to VCC for default 0.5 ms soft start time.
This pin is connected to the auxiliary internal linear regulator. This pin can be connected to the output of the regulator to provide bias for the IC.
This pin configures the soft-start time. Connect a capacitor from this pin to ground to program the soft-start time. Tie this pin to VCC for default 0.5 ms soft start time.
This pin is connected to the inverting input of the feedback error amplifier and should be connected to a properly selected resistor divider from VOUT to ground to set the output voltage.
This pin is connected to the analog ground of the IC. Connect this pin to the PCB ground plain.
This pin is an open-drain power-good output. It can be pulled up with a resistor to VCC or a digital rail for proper function.

This pin sets the switching frequency. Connect a resistor from this pin to ground to set the switching frequency from 200 kHz to 800 kHz. Connect this pin to VCC for default 400kHz switching frequency.
This pin is connected to the output of internal linear regulators and provides bias for the IC including the gate driver. Connect this pin to ground with a decoupling ceramic capacitor.
This pin is the enable pin. It is high voltage tolerant and so can be directly connected to VIN.
This is the bottom thermal pad. It should be connected to the AGND and to the PCB ground plain with thermal vias.

logical_pins.name: PGND
logical_pins.description: Power ground. Connected to the source of the integrated low-side FET.

logical_pins.name: SW
logical_pins.description: Phase node of the regulator. Connected to the source of the high-side FET and the drain of the low-side FET.

logical_pins.name: VIN
logical_pins.description: Power supply input. Connected to the drain of the integrated high-side FET and input of internal linear regulator.

logical_pins.name: BST
logical_pins.description: Bootstrap circuit supply pin. Connect to SW with a capacitor to provide bias for the high-side FET gate driver.

logical_pins.name: EBIAS
logical_pins.description: Auxiliary internal linear regulator input. Can be connected to the output of the regulator to provide bias for the IC.

logical_pins.name: SS
logical_pins.description: Soft-start time configuration. Connect a capacitor to ground to program soft-start time.

logical_pins.name: FB
logical_pins.description: Inverting input of the feedback error amplifier. Connected to a resistor divider from VOUT to ground.

logical_pins.name: AGND
logical_pins.description: Analog ground of the IC. Connect to the PCB ground plain.

logical_pins.name: PG
logical_pins.description: Open-drain power-good output. Can be pulled up with a resistor to VCC or a digital rail.

logical_pins.name: FS
logical_pins.description: Switching frequency setting pin. Connect a resistor to ground to set frequency from 200 kHz to 800 kHz.

logical_pins.name: VCC
logical_pins.description: Output of internal linear regulators. Provides bias for the IC including gate driver. Connect to ground with a decoupling capacitor.

logical_pins.name: EN
logical_pins.description: Enable pin. High voltage tolerant and can be directly connected to VIN.

logical_pins.name: PAD
logical_pins.description: Bottom thermal pad. Should be connected to the AGND and to the PCB ground plain with thermal vias.
